item.title: Whyalla locals call for more certainty around steelworks' future amid news of final bidders
item.description: Whyalla locals welcome more certainty about the future ownership of the city's steelworks, but say it is still not enough to warrant business investment and workers coming back to the Spencer Gulf.

item.title: 'Optimism' as Walk for Truth ends despite no formal commitment from PM
item.description: The Walk for Truth ended at Parliament House today and while there was not a firm commitment from the PM, many felt the response was a 'positive move'.

item.title: Death in custody of Patience Kuzmins leaves family heartbroken, inquest hears
item.description: The family of a young Indigenous woman who died in a NSW jail has told an inquest that she was a talented artist who generously supported anyone in need. The inquest is examining Corrective Services NSW's search and body scan practices.

item.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: NT police say cause of Kumanjayi White's death in custody in Alice Springs still unknown
item.description: The Northern Territory police commissioner says the cause of 24-year-old Kumanjayi White's death in custody remains undetermined, with the criminal case against the officers who restrained him now closed.

item.title: NASA reveals plans for living on the moon
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Five people trapped in flooded cave in Laos found alive but two still missing
item.description: Five of the seven people, who entered the cave in central Laos more than a week ago, have been found alive. Rescuers are still searching for two.

item.title: Rescuers find villagers trapped in Laos cave after landslide
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: State of Origin quick hits: James Tedesco scores Blues' match-winner, Maroons star Kalyn Ponga sent off
item.description: The Blues leave it late, the Maroons finish with 12 players, while the bunker draws the ire of players, commentators and spectators alike. Here are five quick hits from State of Origin I.

item.title: State of Origin: Kalyn Ponga shattered by send-off in Game I as Ashley Klein overrules bunker
item.description: Kalyn Ponga is left feeling responsible for Queensland's Origin I collapse as the decision to send him off leaves several greats shocked.

item.title: Trump says US 'not satisfied' with Iran peace deal proposals
item.description: It comes as the White House rejects a draft deal being reported by Iranian state TV as a "fabrication".

item.title: New York and New Jersey investigate 2026 FIFA World Cup ticket prices
item.description: In a joint statement, New Jersey's attorney-general accused the football governing body of having turned "buying a ticket to the World Cup into a gauntlet of confusion, fake scarcity, and impossibly high prices".

item.title: Put your Australian TV show knowledge to the test with this picture quiz
item.description: How well do you know your Neighbours from your Deadloch? Take our single-frame Australian TV quiz to find out.

item.title: Explain the Internet: The Backrooms captured cyberspace and now it's infecting cinemas
item.description: Forget about slashers and body horror — the next evolution of fright stems from an fluorescent-lit room.

item.title: Israel warns all residents in southern Lebanon to leave
item.description: It is the first time Israel has made such a sweeping demand since US President Donald Trump announced in April a ceasefire had been imposed in Lebanon.

item.title: Labor is quietly confident the battery boom will help prevent power price shocks
item.description: Within the government, there's quiet confidence the battery boom will continue to gather pace and help shield Australia from both global shocks and domestic demand surges, driving prices even lower.

item.title: Australia ranks third in the world for battery capacity and WA is leading the charge
item.description: Western Australia in particular is proving to the world that grid-scale batteries are no longer a complementary technology but can play a central role in power grids.

item.title: State of Origin: Ethan Strange and James Tedesco star in Blues' 22-20 win over Maroons
item.description: When the Blues needed heroes to get on top of the Maroons in State of Origin I, they turned to a debutante and the oldest player on the field.

item.title: Torquay artist's willie wagtail paintings taking flight around the Surf Coast and beyond
item.description: A Victorian artist paints hundreds of willie wagtails a week to give away locally and now they're popping up all over the world.

item.title: Upper house member Ruth Forrest calls for apology after personal health request leaked to media
item.description: A Tasmanian independent politician is calling for an apology after her personal health-related request via email to the government was leaked to a media outlet.

item.title: One in 10 Australian teens relying on withdrawal method and half not using condoms as STIs rise
item.description: A long-running survey of Australian teens has identified falling condom use and unwanted sex as major concerns for young people starting relationships.

item.title: Matthew Perry's assistant jailed for role in Friends actor's death
item.description: Matthew Perry's live-in personal assistant, who had a central role in the Friends star's descent into ketamine addiction and injected him with the fatal dose of the drug, has been sentenced to three years and five months in prison.

item.title: Can farmers become less reliant on synthetic fertiliser? Some are trying
item.description: A war thousands of kilometres away from Australia's farms is quietly reshaping what crops are planted and what will be available at supermarkets in the future.

item.title: Regional employers, academics say one-size-fits-all migration policy will decimate critical workforces
item.description: Amid ongoing discussion about Australia's immigration intake and migration policies, regional residents say many industries in the bush cannot survive without overseas workers.

item.title: Load limits on Victorian bridges 'wreaking havoc' as councils stuggle to fund maintenance
item.description: Councils across regional Victoria say they are being forced to ban trucks from some bridges because they cannot afford to fix them to keep them safe.

item.title: Stolen Generations brothers share their story ahead of Reconciliation Week
item.description: Iain Seymour never knew he was Aboriginal until finding out he was adopted three years ago and then meeting his brother Allen Edwards, the son of a woman who played a key part in setting up a poignant memorial to the long-term effects of the Stolen Generations. Warning: This story contains images of Indigenous people who have died.

item.title: Rainy weather drenching Australia's east could turn May into one of the wettest on record
item.description: A series of low-pressure systems are drenching Australia this week, quickly turning this May into one of the wettest on record for parts of the country's east.

item.title: 'Second wave' of algal bloom forecast for SA's Spencer Gulf
item.description: Modelling predicts that the algal bloom that has plagued South Australia's coastlines since March 2025 will be moving further through the Spencer Gulf in the next few months.

item.title: Legal action filed against federal environment minister's decision to allow land clearing at NT station
item.description: The federal environment minister's decision to wave through plans to bulldoze almost 3,000 hectares — an area 10 times the size of Sydney's CBD — of habitat, home to threatened species, will be challenged in the Federal Court.

item.title: Billy Slater refuses to guarantee Reece Walsh State of Origin recall despite Queensland's collapse
item.description: Queensland's coach bats away questions about the notable absence of Brisbane's star fullback after seeing his side cough up a 20-point lead.

item.title: French Open: Alejandro Davidovich Fokina's coach quits via text as Novak Djokovic survives stiff test
item.description: Alenajdro Davidovich Fokina's French Open comes to an end shortly after his coach flees to Miami after claiming he was unwell after a lunch catch-up.

item.title: Wanted alleged killer Anthony Pele's social media posts 'incredibly valuable' to police
item.description: A wanted Sydney underworld figure may have unwittingly helped police after posting social media messages mocking his underworld adversaries from overseas.

item.title: French Open handshake drama spices up women's draw as Elena Rybakina eliminated
item.description: There is no love lost between Tamara Korpatsch and Wang Xinyu after a testy match as Elena Rybakina's hopes of another grand slam title go up in smoke.

item.title: The most influential female composers in history and today
item.description: From Hildegard von Bingen to Hildur Guðnadóttir, here are some of the most influential women of the music world in history and today.

item.title: Hamas confirms that Israeli air strike killed its new military leader in Gaza
item.description: An Israeli air strike in Gaza has killed new Hamas leader Mohammed Odeh, his wife and two of his children, the militant group has confirmed.

item.title: Perth mother who lost two children to rare genetic condition wants mandatory tests
item.description: A Perth woman who lost two children to an extremely rare medical condition calls for mandatory genetic testing for children whose cause of death is uncertain.

item.title: Hugh Marks denies 'threat' to force ABC news director out of role
item.description: ABC managing director Hugh Marks says he did not "make threats" to terminate departing news boss Justin Stevens, as senators question his sudden departure.

item.title: Buses 'unsafe and late' in Cairns as regional public transport 'struggles' to match population growth
item.description: Amid a spate of assaults at bus stops in Far North Queensland, advocates say there's a need for change.

item.title: Federal government to sue 3M Australia over firefighting foam that contained PFAS
item.description: The Commonwealth is launching its largest-ever legal claim, suing the manufacturer of a firefighting foam containing PFAS chemicals that contaminated 28 Defence Force bases across Australia.

item.title: Four EzyMart Sydney stores among 17 retailers shut over illegal tobacco, vape sale claims
item.description: The major Australian retail company will part ways with an independent operator after several Sydney stores were shut down for allegedly selling illegal tobacco and vapes.

item.title: Freshwater development triggers calls for protections of significant trees
item.description: After several failed court challenges, a council has angered a community with its approval to remove a significant tree to make way for a duplex development, but experts say local governments need to start paying more attention to residents' concerns.

item.title: A WA charity hay run was in doubt until school students and Lions Clubs stepped in to help
item.description: Emergency hay deliveries to drought-stricken areas resume after thousands of dollars in donations from school students and charity groups.

item.title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi breaks IPL sixes record with stunning knock against Pat Cummins and Sunrisers Hyderabad
item.description: Australia's captain feels the brunt of the pain as Indian sensational Vaibhav Sooryavanshi continues his phenomenal IPL form.

item.title: US Justice Department launches criminal probe into E Jean Carroll, source says
item.description: The probe is focused on whether Carroll committed perjury in testimony tied to two civil lawsuits that she won against Mr Trump — one tied to ‌her allegations that he sexually abused her in a New York department store.

item.title: Ali Khamenei's Iranian home a site of mourning three months after his death
item.description: Outside the Tehran compound that was home to now-dead Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei, hundreds of his supporters gather every night to mourn the only leader many of them have ever known.

item.title: Man fined for distributing pamphlets targeting MP Allegra Spender prior to federal election
item.description: A finance executive has been fined $30,000 over unauthorised pamphlets that targeted the Wentworth MP in the lead up to the federal election.

item.title: Small habits that can lower your grocery bill
item.description: Small changes to the way we approach our meal planning can make a big difference to the overall cost.

item.title: Dean Solomon 'won't consider' chasing full-time Essendon coaching job, denies reports of Brad Scott rift
item.description: Despite being Brad Scott's immediate replacement, Essendon interim coach Dean Solomon is not throwing up his hands for the full-time role any time soon.

item.title: Greens likely to play ball with Labor's timeline for capital gains and negative gearing bill
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers introduced the bill to parliament on Thursday, which the government hopes to pass before the winter recess.

item.title: Coroner hoping to begin inquest into Hillcrest Primary School jumping castle tragedy in September
item.description: A coronial inquest into the deaths of six children at a Tasmanian primary school could begin in September, nearly five years after the tragedy occurred.

item.title: Man charged over alleged hit-and-run in Buderim that left 13yo boy with critical injuries
item.description: The young cyclist was riding at dusk when he was hit by a ute that allegedly failed to stop and left him with critical leg injuries.

item.title: Housing investors increasingly older, wealthier: RBA
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: National bushfire outlook puts areas of NSW and WA at heightened risk
item.description: A national bushfire outlook forecasts above-average daytime and overnight temperatures for winter and an increased risk for NSW and WA.

item.title: Hantavirus cruise ship passengers have WA quarantine period extended
item.description: Six people who were on a cruise ship that was hit by an outbreak of hantavirus have had their quarantine period in the outer Perth suburb of Bullsbrook extended until June 23.

item.title: Darwin Street Art Festival highlights the Top End city's claim as the mural capital of Australia
item.description: Artists say Darwin has surpassed Melbourne as Australia's street art capital, with work underway on a fresh round of murals across the Top End city.

item.title: Strengthened laws for coercive control, family violence put forward by Tasmanian government
item.description: New laws designed to strengthen family violence protections in Tasmania include an extension to the statutory limitation period for coercive control offences, and a "nation-leading" levy which would be paid by offenders.

item.title: Officers down drone carrying $1 million worth of drugs, syringes into Townsville prison
item.description: Queensland Corrective Services say the drone was carrying illicit drugs, syringes and a mobile phone.

item.title: Robert Black Farmer refused parole over 2005 attack on Lauren Huxley in Sydney home
item.description: Robert Black Farmer was handed a 24-year sentence over the attempted murder of Lauren Huxley at her Northmead home in 2005.

item.title: Police arrest man over alleged 1994 armed robbery at Chadstone Shopping Centre
item.description: A man who allegedly carried out an armed robbery and shot three men at Chadstone Shopping Centre in 1994 has been arrested by police in Queensland.

item.title: Cleanaway fined $1.1m, convicted over 2014 double-fatal Adelaide truck crash
item.description: Almost 12 years after a Cleanaway truck killed two people and injured two others, the company has been sentenced in South Australia's Supreme Court.

item.title: Lawyer says alleged childcare paedophile Joshua Brown will admit to crimes
item.description: Joshua Brown, who has been charged with 156 offences, including sexual assault, has appeared in the Melbourne Magistrates' Court via video link.

item.title: Lawyers expect will disputes to escalate with over $5 trillion to be bequeathed in coming decades
item.description: A significant proportion of us believe that an inheritance will be the key to our future financial security, but most families have not even discussed what is in the will, and 40 per cent do not have one at all, a recent survey shows.

item.title: Investigation finds smuggled drugs cause of inmate Phi Long Dang's death at Fulham prison
item.description: A 35-year-old inmate at Victoria's Fulham Correctional Centre died of a drug overdose after consuming drugs smuggled into the jail, a coronial investigation has found.

item.title: Hudson Young finds method in the madness for long-awaited State of Origin arrival
item.description: It took three years and four series, but when Hudson Young was given the chance to impose his unorthodox abilities on State of Origin football, he didn't hesitate to harness the chaos for New South Wales.

item.title: Lawyers flag legal action over Tiwi Island town manager's decision to block bail of alleged murderer
item.description: Lawyers prepare legal action against a Tiwi Islands town manager who blocked an alleged murderer from being granted bail to the islands, where his potential return has already provoked rioting.

item.title: Canavan lines up another stunt as Albanese lays a tax trap
item.description: This week a few people got a grilling at Senate estimates, the tax debate heated up and whispers of a new centre-right party fluttered through the press gallery.

item.title: Victoria's political donations cap to double under proposed Electoral Act changes
item.description: The cap on political donations in Victoria will be at least doubled and public funding for political parties increased under government proposals to rewrite the regime struck out by the High Court.

item.title: Man who killed Darwin mum Sheena Fairfield indefinitely detained due to mental impairment
item.description: Sheena Fairfield was stabbed to death outside her family home more than three years ago. Her killer will now spend 20 years in supervised custody.

item.title: Brisbane's $3.6b Olympic stadium faces court challenge by residents
item.description: The Queensland government wants site work to begin on Brisbane's $3.6 billion Olympic stadium on Monday as residents plan a court challenge and Indigenous protesters refuse to move.

item.title: Inquest finds RAAF serviceman Gary Sargeant's death was preventable
item.description: A coroner has found that the death of an RAAF serviceman could have been prevented, including when he left a military health centre during a mental health crisis. Warning: This story contains discussion of suicide and mental health issues.

item.title: State of Origin referee Ashley Klein's decision to send off Kalyn Ponga backed by NRL
item.description: The NRL supports Ashley Klein's controversial decision to send off Kalyn Ponga, saying the State of Origin referee got the call right.

item.title: 'Unfair' Women's Origin timing criticised as Blues sweep Maroons aside
item.description: The captains of New South Wales and Queensland question the timing of the Women's State of Origin series after the Blues completed an historic clean sweep.

item.title: Thursday finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Diver describes extreme and hostile claustrophobia inside the Laos cave
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Hawks to go to AFL over Watson's disallowed goal
item.description: Hawthorn expects a quick AFL adjustment after Nick Watson's goal at half-time was disallowed against St Kilda in the Hawks' win.

item.title: Coronial inquest probes NSW Police staffing levels and mental health support
item.description: An inquest into the death of a NSW Police recruit who was less than three months into the job hears differing opinions about whether it was related to his work.

item.title: Former police officer says he got into bed with woman but denies raping her, court hears
item.description: A former South Australian police officer says he got into bed with a woman while she was sleeping and caressed her thigh soon after she rejected his advances, but denies raping her, a court has heard.

item.title: AFP Commissioner Krissy Barrett defends Ben Roberts-Smith airport arrest
item.description: The Australian Federal Police has detailed intelligence surrounding Ben Roberts-Smith's arrest, pushing back against criticisms of its public nature.

item.title: Construction looms on contentious Olympic Brisbane stadium
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Iran fires missile at US air base in Kuwait in retaliation for American attack
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he is not yet satisfied by negotiations to end the war in Iran, and the US is not discussing easing sanctions, which is one of Tehran's key demands.

item.title: At least 16 students dead as fire tears through Kenyan girls school
item.description: The blaze reportedly started when a student set a mattress on fire inside the school's dormitory, with a further 79 students taken to hospital with injuries.

item.title: Trump says Murdoch indicated he would 'handle' newspaper's Epstein story
item.description: In refiling his lawsuit against The Wall Street Journal, the US president alleges that Rupert Murdoch indicated that a story about his ties to Jeffrey Epstein would not be published.

item.title: How to grow garlic in a small garden
item.description: In April 2020, I put my first cloves into the ground. Here's what I learnt over the next five years of trying — and often failing — to grow good garlic.

item.title: ABC news quiz: A drone drama, a 'dream job' and an old insult making a comeback
item.description: Your hopes of passing might come crashing down like so many drones in a harbour. But it's still worth a try.

item.title: Johnny Galvatron left his band to make video games. Now, Mixtape explores 90s nostalgia through music
item.description: Melbourne's Johnny Galvatron gave up the rockstar life to become a BAFTA-winning game developer. Now, his studio's latest release, Mixtape, re-imagines the music game as an eclectic playlist of genres and mechanics.

item.title: Small business Yarn'n says Labor's capital gains tax plan 'handcuffs' ambition
item.description: Small business owners are urging Labor to rethink more than just the eligibility thresholds for capital gains tax concessions.

item.title: Australians have 'startlingly' low awareness of controllable dementia risk factors
item.description: Too many Australians still see dementia as an inevitability, according to new data, but there is a list of steps you can take to reduce the risks.

item.title: Treasury boss says top income earners would have paid $400,000 more in tax if Labor plan was in place
item.description: Commonwealth Treasury secretary Jenny Wilkinson says the average top income earner benefited from the existing investment tax arrangements to the tune of $700,000 over their lifetime, versus just $5,700 for median income earners.

item.title: In the outback town of Wilcannia, hope lies in the classroom and on Country
item.description: Schools in the outback often struggle to maintain consistent attendance rates, but the Wilcannia community is going the extra mile to get kids into the classroom.

item.title: Warning issued as UK hits 35C in spring and records tumble
item.description: As the mercury climbed around the United Kingdom this week and new May maximums were recorded in multiple areas, the once-welcome sunshine is sparking alarm among many people.

item.title: Government faces questions about first payment to Nauru under NZYQ deportee deal
item.description: Details of the secretive NZYQ resettlement deal have emerged, including a breakdown of the government's first payment.

item.title: Labor campaigned on 'integrity' so the NACC should be enjoying public trust by now
item.description: In an extraordinary irony, the National Anti-Corruption Commission has become best known for "own goals", rocked by internal scandals and attracting a litany of complaints.

item.title: Austrian man jailed for 15 years for Taylor Swift concert terror plot
item.description: A man who plotted but ultimately failed to launch a terrorist attack at Taylor Swift concert in Vienna, forcing the pop star to cancel three performances, is sentenced to 15 years behind bars.

item.title: Aggreko building Australia's largest hybrid power plant in outback Queensland in light of CopperString delays
item.description: An international energy company is building Australia's largest off-grid renewable hybrid power facility, as a state politician criticises the need for it.

item.title: Private landholders hand three blocks in Tasmania to Aboriginal Land Council
item.description: It has been two decades since substantial land hand-backs in Tasmania, so private owners are doing it instead. Near Hobart, a piece of land that was once a dumping ground is being restored.

item.title: Australia focused on making winning start at Valladolid leg of World Championship rugby sevens
item.description: The threat of relegation hangs over the Australian men's rugby sevens squad ahead of the final two rounds of the World Championship series.

item.title: Patient advocates hope review will improve travel subsidy rates and response times
item.description: Kevin O'Neill says Queensland's patient travel subsidy scheme left him stranded with a dangerous medical condition 300 kilometres from home. Patient advocates say it is one of a litany of failures they hope will be fixed in a review of the scheme.

item.title: Letter alleges 'unlawful conspiracy' to oust new APY Lands general manager
item.description: The new manager of a remote South Australian Aboriginal land corporation has alleged there is an "unlawful conspiracy" to oust him, but the board says his position has already been terminated.

item.title: Officeworks to offshore hundreds of Sydney and Melbourne jobs to India and Philippines
item.description: The Australian office products retailer is planning to offshore hundreds of jobs to India and the Philippines, joining a growing trend of businesses hoping to find more highly skilled white-collar workers overseas.

item.title: Major wine retailer Endeavour Group retreats from production amid industry pressure
item.description: Endeavour Group, which owns Dan Murphy's and BWS, announces it will step away from some key vineyards and wineries in South Australia, Victoria and Tasmania, and close a major South Australian bottling plant.

item.title: Celine Cremer search volunteers to look for missing Tasmanian man Peter Willoughby
item.description: A group of volunteers, which played a major role in finding the remains of a lost Belgian tourist earlier this year, will today begin searching for another missing person in Tasmania's wilderness.

item.title: Jannik Sinner exits French Open in five-set loss to Juan Manuel Cerúndolo in Paris
item.description: Jannik Sinner's French Open campaign is over after the world number one struggles with illness in a shock second-round defeat to Juan Manuel Cerúndolo.

item.title: 'Eco village' project proposed as solution to Lismore housing crisis languishes
item.description: It was lauded as a solution to the housing crisis, but those involved say plans to build an "eco village" in northern New South Wales have gone awry.

item.title: Victoria Park advocates urge Queensland government to stop stadium work until Indigenous heritage challenges assessed
item.description: Excavators and fencing began appearing at the Brisbane park earlier this week, but the federal government has not yet assessed challenges under the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Heritage Protection Act.

item.title: 'Stunning' weekend weather forecast for south-east Queensland before cool change
item.description: Queensland residents can expect a reprieve from the recent "wet and stormy" weather this weekend, with cooler-than-average temperatures on the way.

item.title: Canberra man jailed over sexual abuse of 'extremely vulnerable' child in 2024
item.description: A man who sexually assaulted a 12-year-old girl after a "chance encounter" at a Canberra shopping centre has been jailed, with the judge finding he took advantage of an "extremely vulnerable" child.

item.title: Darwin Harbour Middle Arm gas and petrochemical precinct could be roadblocked by endangered herb
item.description: The NT government is arguing its planned mega Middle Arm gas industry precinct will be needed to help shore up Australia's future fuel and energy supplies — but it has just identified that a tiny endangered herb could be growing in the way.

item.title: Australia's Daria Kasatkina sets up French Open clash with Aryna Sabalenka in third round
item.description: Daria Kasatkina will face world number one Aryna Sabalenka in the third round as fellow Australian Kimberly Birrell departs the singles draw in Paris.

item.title: Controversial Sunshine Coast housing project receives federal approval
item.description: Up to 12,000 homes could be added to the fast-growing region after a major hurdle was cleared for the contentious Aura South development to proceed.

item.title: Australian Border Force seize 20 million illegal vapes since January 2024 as crackdown continues
item.description: More than 20 million illegal vapes, with a street value of just over $1 billion, have been seized by law enforcement authorities since Australia's vaping reforms came into effect.

item.title: Auction clearance rates at 2022 housing downturn levels, ASX up — as it happened
item.description: Auction clearance rates have fallen to levels not seen since the 2022 housing downturn. The ASX closed up 1.6 per cent on hopes of a Middle East peace deal.

item.title: From Kneecap to Ecca Vandal and Genesis Owusu — these are May's best albums
item.description: From long-awaited comebacks to First Nations glam rock and genre-defying polymaths, May was stacked with excellent new releases.

item.title: Yindjibarndi leader not ready to accept $150m native title payout from Andrew Forrest's Fortescue
item.description: More than a fortnight after winning the largest native title compensation payout ever in Australia, Pilbara leader Michael Woodley returns to his country.

item.title: NSW skipper Isabelle Kelly 'speechless' after Blues make Women's State of Origin history
item.description: NSW captain Isabelle Kelly praises her Blues teammates after they complete a historic 3-0 State of Origin whitewash against Queensland.

item.title: ACCC sues Amazon over alleged non-compliance with button battery warnings in kids backpacks
item.description: The consumer watchdog is suing Amazon alleging it sold children's unicorn backpacks on its online marketplace that failed to comply with mandatory button battery safety warnings.

item.title: Diphtheria vaccine push in WA's north as authorities battle 'disease of the past' disinformation
item.description: Health authorities in Western Australia's far north say they are battling disinformation and misconceptions amid a vaccination drive to contain a diphtheria outbreak.

item.title: Sarah Gigante to miss Giro d'Italia Women through injury, Jai Hindley fourth in men's race with two stages remaining
item.description: Sarah Gigante, who finished third at last year's Giro d'Italia Women, will not take to the start line on Saturday after succumbing to a leg injury, continuing her horror run of injury.

item.title: Comedian Will Gibb on the perils of TikTok fame
item.description: As a comedian with legions of fans on social media, Will Gibb wishes he didn't still feel the need to legitimise himself. But he does.

item.title: Claims funding promise to cancer patients missing from Tasmanian state budget
item.description: A cancer survivor who stood alongside the Tasmanian premier at a 2025 election promise announcement says he is disappointed that the hospitals investment appears to be "on the back burner."

item.title: New drivers inducted in Rockhampton for the last working Purrey steam tram in the world
item.description: Steam-powered trams ran in Rockhampton for 30 years until 1939 and are now displayed at a volunteer museum, between their Sunday and school holiday trips.

item.title: Kate Conroy appointed inaugural general manager of Australian AI Safety Institute
item.description: A philosopher and Royal Australian Air Force reservist has been chosen to lead the federal government's efforts to keep Australians safe from the harms of AI.

item.title: Two senior NSW ministers suspended from upper house over failure to release documents
item.description: Government frontbenchers John Graham and Penny Sharpe have been suspended from the upper house over failing to produce documents demanded by the opposition.

item.title: Jannik Sinner's French Open loss to Juan Manuel Cerúndolo leaves rivals stunned
item.description: It seemed a foregone conclusion Jannik Sinner would win the French Open, which is why his meltdown amid the Paris heat wave was so unexpected.

item.title: Afghan Refugee Women's XI Cricket Tour of Darwin spotlights need for gender equality in sport
item.description: After the Taliban banned women's sport in Afghanistan, most of the women's cricket team ended up relocating to Australia. Now, they're on tour in the Northern Territory — "a happy ending" after a traumatic journey.

item.title: Five arrests as police and protesters clash at Victoria Park amid Olympic stadium dispute
item.description: Five people were taken into custody and a protest camp was broken up at Victoria Park ahead of construction beginning on the $3.6 billion Olympic stadium development.

item.title: John Farnham tribute concert to be held in September at Melbourne's Rod Laver Arena
item.description: Jimmy Barnes, Tina Arena and Human Nature will take the stage to honour Australian legend John Farnham at a Melbourne tribute concert later this year.

item.title: White House launches alien-themed website that allows users to track arrests made by ICE
item.description: The website features a space-inspired background but its real purpose is to highlight the work of ICE.

item.title: Victorian firefighters consider restarting PFAS case against 3M in wake of federal lawsuit
item.description: The federal government's $2 billion lawsuit against "forever chemicals" manufacturer 3M has Fire Rescue Victoria considering whether it should relaunch its own legal proceedings.

item.title: Residents in Lebanon's south caught in the middle as fighting again intensifies
item.description: New waves of missile strikes and vast advisories from the Israeli government to evacuate have left residents in Lebanon's south uncertain of where to turn.

item.title: What the fight over London data centre plans tells us about the AI backlash
item.description: Last year in the US alone, projects collectively worth $200 billion were scuppered or delayed as communities protested the construction of new data centres promised to deliver the AI of the future.

item.title: Trump officials push for $250 note featuring US president's face for nation's anniversary
item.description: The decision will be made by the US House and Senate, but reports say Donald Trump's supporters are pushing to expedite the process.

item.title: Friends, loyal listeners gather for James Valentine's public memorial
item.description: A celebration of ABC presenter James Valentine's life has taken place at a public service at Sydney's Town Hall, where the memorial included contributions from colleagues, family friends and music icons.

item.title: Rising costs threaten eisteddfods across Australia
item.description: The Centralian Eisteddfod Council has raised the alarm about funding challenges, which could mean the event will cease after next year.

item.title: KPMG boss resigns over mishandled whistleblower allegations
item.description: KPMG Australia chief executive Andrew Yates has stepped down amid a scandal involving audit partners accessing confidential client documents.

item.title: Blue Origin New Glenn rocket explodes on launch pad during engine-firing test
item.description: Billionaire Jeff Bezos's Blue Origin says all personnel are accounted for after experiencing an "anomaly", a term commonly used by rocket companies to describe a launch failure or explosion.

item.title: Newly discovered ancient wasp species named after F1 driver Oscar Piastri
item.description: Biologists have named the wasp species Gwesped piastrii, honouring Australian Formula 1 driver Oscar Piastri.

item.title: Economic gap between older and younger generations on track to reach record levels
item.description: According to research from the Actuaries Institute, inequality between the younger and older generations is widening and could reach record levels within years.

item.title: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket explodes during test fire
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Family in 'forever home' pleads for changes to Jervis Bay Road upgrade
item.description: After years spent modifying their Shoalhaven property to accommodate their son's special care needs, the Taylors fear noise, lights and disruption from a major road project could affect his health.

item.title: Canberra man refused bail over alleged shooting, ramming incident in Belconnen
item.description: The 20-year-old's defence lawyer said his client was needed at a barber shop, but Magistrate James Stewart said his circumstances did not meet the threshold for bail.

item.title: Taylors Wines considers moving SA business overseas if tax changes go through
item.description: The managing director of Taylors Wines in South Australia is considering leaving the country in response to the federal government's proposed tax changes.

item.title: Victorian coroner finds earlier hospital transfer could have prevented baby's death after home birth
item.description: A Victorian coroner has found a baby's death could have been avoided if his mother had been transferred to hospital hours earlier, after signs of an obstructed labour.

item.title: Parramatta Eels reveal Mitchell Moses injury outlook ahead of State of Origin II
item.description: Parramatta do not have any concerns with the handling of Mitchell Moses's fitness in the NSW Blues set-up, as the Eels skipper faces a race against the clock to play before Game II of State of Origin.

item.title: Prisoner loses battle to freeze her eggs while serving 10-year sentence
item.description: The Court of Appeal knocked back the woman's attempt to have her eggs frozen while she serves a ten year prison sentence.

item.title: Victorian state MP Jacinta Ermacora apologises for mistakenly saying alleged koala killer had died
item.description: State MP Jacinta Ermacora says "they were misinformed and passed that misinformation on".

item.title: Drag queen Pattie Gonia says Patagonia has launched trademark suit. Here's what we know
item.description: A drag queen and a major outdoor clothing brand have a fight over trademarks. Here's the feud explained.

item.title: Friends, loyal listeners gather for James Valentine's public memorial
item.description: The veteran broadcaster's family, friends and loyal listeners gathered in Sydney Town Hall to pay tribute to the 64-year-old who died last month, two years after he was diagnosed with cancer.

item.title: Erin Patterson's appeal against mushroom murder conviction set for August
item.description: Erin Patterson's appeal against her triple murder conviction will be heard in August, Victoria's Supreme Court has confirmed, with the director of public prosecutions also challenging the length of her jail term.

item.title: FIFA World Cup selection for Michael Woud forces wedding postponement
item.description: Auckland FC goalkeeper Michael Woud has had to put his wedding plans on hold after being selected in New Zealand's squad for the World Cup, but he says taking part in soccer's biggest tournament is not a bad reason to cancel.

item.title: Violent teen on probation before causing fatal crash that killed friend
item.description: A Melbourne teenager who had been on probation for just nine days when he caused a deadly, high-speed crash is also facing court over a bashing that left a man with life-long disabilities.

item.title: Wild weather set to slam Perth and southern WA over long weekend
item.description: Perth and large parts of Western Australia's south are preparing for "an unusually strong and intense winter system" over the WA Day long weekend, with destructive wind gusts and heavy rainfall expected.

item.title: Plummeting gold price impacts Australian dollar as Iran war pushes it into bear market
item.description: The gold price is now plunging after the precious metal's surge to a record high this year.

item.title: California teenager wins Scripps National Spelling Bee with 'bromocriptine'
item.description: Correctly spelling 32 obscure words in just a minute and a half has won 14-year-old Shrey Parikh this year's Scripps National Spelling Bee.

item.title: US prosecutor denies launching criminal investigation into E Jean Carroll
item.description: Chicago's top federal prosecutor has denied his office has launched a criminal investigation into E Jean Carroll, who accused Donald Trump of raping her in the mid-1990s.

item.title: Are you allowed to make deepfakes of the prime minister?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: NSW coroner absolves police of blame for pursuit that led to death on Pacific Motorway
item.description: NSW Deputy State Coroner David O'Neil finds police acted within reason during a pursuit that led to a man being killed on the Pacific Motorway, north of Byron Bay.

item.title: AFL backs controversial decision to disallow Hawks' goal against Saints
item.description: A controversial umpiring decision that disallowed Hawthorn star Nick Watson's goal against St Kilda has been backed by the AFL.

item.title: Mouse plague taskforce to help WA country schools battling infestation
item.description: COVID-style cleaning measures have returned as WA's ongoing mouse plague spreads across multiple country schools.

item.title: Award-winning volunteer Jamal Box bailed on child-abuse image charges
item.description: A former police staffer and lauded SES volunteer is granted bail on a charge of possessing child-abuse images.

item.title: Angela Jones-Blayney defends appointment of 'Pink Lady' mentee to Parramatta council
item.description: A Western Sydney local government executive undermined recruitment processes to hire a woman she mentored for years and socialised with as part of a networking group dubbed the Pink Ladies.

item.title: Bumper year at Northern Territory's biggest bull sale delivers average price of nearly $5,000 a head
item.description: The Northern Territory's biggest bull sale has recorded one of its strongest results, despite high fuel prices and a big wet season.

item.title: Former prime minister Tony Abbott elected unopposed as Liberal Party president
item.description: Former prime minister Tony Abbott says he is ready to serve the Liberal Party in its time of 'existential crisis' upon his return to the political stage.

item.title: New Tennis Australia CEO Andrew Abdo unlikely to be grand slam boss
item.description: Incoming Tennis Australia CEO Andrew Abdo appears unlikely to take on the double workload of his predecessor Craig Tiley, by running both the national organisation and the Australian Open tournament.

item.title: Man who claimed mother stole Dreamworld disaster compensation left feeling let down
item.description: A man who accused his mother of stealing compensation he received after the Dreamworld Thunder River Rapids ride disaster believes the legal system has let him down after a case against her has been discontinued by prosecutors.

item.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Cristian Volpato switches allegiance to Socceroos from Italy
item.description: The Socceroos have been boosted ahead of the FIFA World Cup, with young midfielder Cristian Volpato switching allegiance from Italy to Australia.

item.title: NT Aboriginal families in court bid to be recognised as traditional owners of Cobourg Peninsula
item.description: Two groups of Indigenous families have claimed in the Federal Court they should be recognised as the traditional owners of the NT's Cobourg Peninsula.

item.title: Former police officer's messages did not amount to 'implied admissions' of rape, court told
item.description: A woman who says she was raped by a former South Australian police officer did not confront her alleged attacker "because she knew it wasn't true", a court has heard.

item.title: Scott calls for high ARC standards, laments missed 'howler' in loss
item.description: Geelong's coach takes aim at the AFL's controversial review technology after yet another blunder cost his side dearly in the loss to Carlton.

item.title: Alleged relationship with high-profile married man raised in Cairns extortion case
item.description: A court has heard a man charged with extortion was "potentially gagged" by his bail terms in an effort to protect a high-profile married man.

item.title: Friday finance with Lin Lin
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Kids still see gambling ads during late night sport under Labor
item.description: The partial ad ban will not prevent children from seeing gambling ads during footy or rugby games, Labor's draft legislation shows.

item.title: Blues rookie in doubt for Origin II after injury in Cronulla's win over Manly
item.description: New South Wales are set to be without exciting hooker Blayke Brailey after he suffered a suspected broken arm in Cronulla's 28-22 win over Manly.

item.title: Romanian President Nicusor Dan calls defence council meeting over 'unprecedented' Russian drone crash
item.description: A Russian drone has struck an apartment building in NATO-member Romania, wounding two people.

item.title: WHO says 906 suspected cases and 223 suspected deaths from Bundibugyo strain of Ebola
item.description: The outbreak is threatening to overwhelm health authorities in the Democratic Republic of Congo, while nine cases have now been confirmed in neighbouring Uganda.

item.title: Kenya arrests eight students on suspicion of arson after deadly fire at Utumishi Girls' Academy Senior School
item.description: The fire, at the Utumishi Girls' Academy Senior School in Gilgil in west-central Kenya, broke out in the early hours of Thursday morning and also injured 79 students.

item.title: Canadian man Kenneth Law, accused of selling lethal chemical online, pleads guilty to aiding suicide
item.description: In 2023, Interpol alerted Australian police that packages sold by the man, which contained a lethal substance, were sent to Australia, prompting authorities to check on the welfare of recipients.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of John Farnham, 60s pop and where to live on the Moon
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

item.title: Best new books out in May from Elizabeth Strout, Francesca Albanese and more
item.description: Sweet teen love, gay football stars, alien abductions and a murderous PhD student bent on revenge — this month's best new releases have it all.

item.title: Top films are more likely to star a Chris than older women. It's part of a long trend
item.description: Films are four times more likely to feature a talking animal lead than a woman over 60. These charts explain the state of film for older women.

item.title: Australians limited to keyhole view of security and government failures before Bondi attack
item.description: For now, Australians seeking to understand any intelligence gaps, failures, missed warnings and government failings before the Bondi massacre are expected to accept a keyhole view.

item.title: Warm winter outlook not bothering ski resorts as 2026 snow season looms
item.description: Alpine business owners and resort managers say they are not overly concerned about a warmer winter forecast, with preparations underway for the 2026 ski season ahead of the King's Birthday long weekend.

item.title: A 'mind-blowing' swim for survival and complex abdomen reconstruction saved Sam after Thursday Island shark attack
item.description: Fourteen-year-old Sam Nai survived a devastating shark attack with a desperate swim to safety, followed by 20 surgeries to reconstruct his abdomen.

item.title: 'Heart ripped out on repeat': Auskick parents say they have been failed by AFL
item.description: The ABC has spoken to several families who say they have been failed by the AFL Integrity Unit following bullying, racism and harassment.

item.title: New artificial homes help Kangaroo Island dunnarts recover after bushfire
item.description: Artificial habitats are giving the Kangaroo Island dunnart a chance at recovery, after bushfires left them largely homeless.

item.title: Do AI-generated images have any place in Australian politics?
item.description: A Victorian state MP's use of an apparently AI-generated image raises questions about whether AI has any place in politics.

item.title: Mount Isa mum calls for more support after birth of premature twins
item.description: Mount Isa mother Kristen Curd spent four months in Townsville after giving birth to premature twin boys, and says the hardest part began when they finally came home.

item.title: First of five survivors rescued from flooded Laos cave
item.description: Rescue divers in Laos have safely evacuated the first of five local villagers who had been trapped in a cave for more than a week by floodwaters.

item.title: Businessman David Penman lays further charges against Hepburn councillor in private prosecution
item.description: A private prosecution launched by a businessman in Victoria's Hepburn Shire has been described as a "nightmare" by a lawyer defending one of two local government figures facing charges.

item.title: Booking.com is being sued in Europe, but Aussie travellers aren't included
item.description: Booking.com is being sued in Europe, and consumer advocates think Aussie travellers might also have a claim.

item.title: Bureau of Meteorology tips mild winter after near-record warm May
item.description: Australia's east is coming to the end of an abnormally warm May, and the heat could be here to stay.

item.title: Brisbane local takes stadium fight to Olympic bosses as corruption fears mount
item.description: Opponents of Brisbane's centrepiece Olympic stadium take their concerns directly to the IOC's headquarters in Switzerland as a critical deadline looms.

item.title: 'Notorious' Gold Coast frontman for offshore shell companies faces extradition to US
item.description: A Queensland jetski salesman who helped set up offshore shell companies embroiled in scandals involving Mexican drug cartels and North Korean arms dealers faces extradition to the United States on money laundering charges.

item.title: The Interview: David Szalay on why AI won't be able to replace authors
item.description: The Booker Prize winner sat down at the Sydney Writers' Festival to ponder the threat of AI to fiction, share some recommendations and consider whether his book was really about masculinity.

item.title: Scientists target rabbit fertility in radical new pest-control plan
item.description: Scientists are using rabbit IVF and gene-editing technology to crash Australia's feral rabbit population by spreading infertility through female rabbits.

item.title: French Open: Alex de Minaur and Novak Djokovic eliminated as seeded stars continue struggles in heat
item.description: Seeded stars continue to tumble at Roland-Garros as both Alex de Minaur and Novak Djokovic's campaigns end in the third round.

item.title: Tributes for big-wave surfer Nathan Bartlett who died at NSW South Coast break
item.description: Tributes mount for Nathan Bartlett, who chased epic waves around the world but died at a rugged spot near Jervis Bay, not far from where he grew up, on Wednesday.

item.title: The Kylie Minogue Netflix doco reminds us her ordinariness is her mystery and superpower
item.description: In the end, a woman who's had wins and losses, terrors and triumphs, and who regularly goes back to stay with family in suburban Melbourne, has become a music legend of her time, and it's not hard to figure out why.

item.title: Whyalla community cautiously optimistic about steelworks sequel
item.description: "We've seen this movie before" is a phrase that sums up the way many people feel about the Whyalla steelworks' financial predicament but the community is hopeful of a promising new sequel.

item.title: Diphtheria cases surged a month before NT Health issued public alert, leaked data shows
item.description: Internal NT Health data leaked to the ABC reveals diphtheria case numbers surged in the NT a month before a public health alert was issued. The data also offers the first public insight into the regions hit hardest by the outbreak.

item.title: Colourism in the Pacific continues as region grapples with history of colonialism
item.description: Skin bleaching and lightening products are widely sold in Tonga, which is part of a region with a long history of colourism.
